connection_factory.c(3) Library Functions Manual connection_factory.c(3)

NAME

connection_factory.c - Shared connection factory for TCP and WebSocket endpoints.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t connection_factory_open (const char *name, const char *input, uint16_t default_port, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx, acip_transport_t **transport_out, connection_endpoint_t *endpoint_out)

Detailed Description

Shared connection factory for TCP and WebSocket endpoints.

Definition in file connection_factory.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t connection_factory_open (const char * name, const char * input, uint16_t default_port, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx, acip_transport_t ** transport_out, connection_endpoint_t * endpoint_out)

Open a transport for a resolved endpoint.

The factory hides the backend-specific setup details:

  • TCP endpoints are resolved and connected with timeout handling
  • WebSocket endpoints use the WebSocket transport constructor

Parameters

name Transport/debug name
input Original endpoint string
default_port Fallback port for bare hostnames
crypto_ctx Optional crypto context to attach to the transport
transport_out Receives the connected transport on success
endpoint_out Optional normalized endpoint output

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Definition at line 75 of file connection_factory.c.

77                                                                                {
78   if (!name || !input || !transport_out) {
79     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid connection factory parameters");
80   }
81 
82   *transport_out = NULL;
83 
84   connection_endpoint_t endpoint = {0};
85   asciichat_error_t resolve_result = connection_endpoint_resolve(input, default_port, &endpoint);
86   if (resolve_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
87     return resolve_result;
88   }
89 
90   acip_transport_t *transport = NULL;
91 
92   switch (endpoint.protocol) {
93   case CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET:
94     transport = acip_websocket_client_transport_create(name, endpoint.input, crypto_ctx);
95     if (!transport) {
96       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_CONNECT, "Failed to create WebSocket transport");
97     }
98     break;
99 
100   case CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_TCP:
101     resolve_result = connection_factory_open_tcp(name, &endpoint, crypto_ctx, &transport);
102     if (resolve_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
103       return resolve_result;
104     }
105     break;
106 
107   default:
108     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unsupported endpoint protocol");
109   }
110 
111   if (endpoint_out) {
112     *endpoint_out = endpoint;
113   }
114   *transport_out = transport;
115   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
116 }




References acip_websocket_client_transport_create(),
    ASCIICHAT_OK, connection_endpoint_resolve(),
    CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_TCP, CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK_CONNECT,
    connection_endpoint_t::input, connection_endpoint_t::protocol,
    and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by server_connection_establish().
